title: M1 manual: DNSSEC + CAA on evals.intentsolutions.io + Porkbun + cosign roundtrip — Jeremy action
description: Jeremy manual action required. Per ISEDC v1 Q1 CISO binding: enable DNSSEC at Porkbun for intentsolutions.io. Pin CAA records to single CA before first signed in-toto Statement against evals.intentsolutions.io/gate-result/v1. After enabled, run cosign verify-attestation round-trip against synthetic Statement to prove the chain works. Off-ramp: if registrar fails, pause M1 — do not ship signed rows against half-hardened namespace.
notes: DNSSEC fully validated 2026-05-12 ~04:25 UTC. DS records live at .io registry (2371 13 2 98F71CBB...). AD bit set on Cloudflare resolver responses (validation chain works end-to-end). CISO binding from ISEDC v1 Q1 SATISFIED: zone signed (DNSKEY KSK+ZSK ECDSAP256), CAA pinned to letsencrypt.org with iodef, evals.intentsolutions.io A→167.86.106.29 propagated, DS at .io registry validates. Next: install cosign + run live verify-attestation round-trip against synthetic Statement to formally close M2→M3 transition gate.
close_reason: M2->M3 transition gate EMPIRICALLY CLEARED 2026-05-12 04:25 UTC. Full chain validated end-to-end against the live, DNSSEC-protected predicate URI namespace: (1) DNSSEC enabled at Porkbun UI by Jeremy; DS records live at .io registry (2371 13 2 ECDSAP256SHA256); AD bit set on Cloudflare resolver responses; (2) CAA pinned (issue+issuewild letsencrypt.org + iodef); (3) evals.intentsolutions.io A record propagated to 1.1.1.1+8.8.8.8; (4) j-rig emit-evidence (PR #46) produces valid in-toto Statement v1 with predicateType https://evals.intentsolutions.io/gate-result/v1; (5) cosign v2.4.1 attest-blob signs against the live URI; (6) cosign verify-blob-attestation returns 'Verified OK'. Local Rekor push deferred (would be permanent + production-track decision); the unsigned envelope path works for non-Rekor consumers. Cosign integration finding: cosign wraps predicate in its own Statement v0.1 outer envelope rather than v1 — this is cosign default behavior and is interoperable with GUAC/Rekor/SLSA tooling. Future work: emit-evidence --predicate-body-only mode for direct cosign integration. No Rekor entry pushed in this validation run; the chain WORKS, it's just not yet pinned to public transparency log.

title: [umbrella] Three-repo convergence: Evidence Bundle as keystone schema
description:
Master umbrella for the convergence vision across intent-eval-lab + audit-harness + j-rig-binary-eval. The architectural insight: the three repos compose via a shared schema (Evidence Bundle), not via package consolidation. audit-harness's polyglot trifecta stays. j-rig's TS monorepo stays. intent-eval-lab grows two new spec modules. Convergence happens at the schema layer.

- IEL-CONV-7: File OpenTelemetry RFC for Agent Rollout Gate Signals (task)

Per-repo work issues live in their respective repos (audit-harness, j-rig-binary-eval, intent-eval-lab) and reference this umbrella in their issue bodies.

Phase scope:
- Phase A: documentation + skeleton (now → 90 days, low intensity, NO feature code)
- Phase B: implementation + first paying customer (90 days)
- Phase C: rollout gate + community (90 days)

title: M1: JSON Schema gate-result.schema.json
description: Author specs/evidence-bundle/v0.1.0-draft/schema/gate-result.schema.json. Validates the predicate body for predicateType https://evals.intentsolutions.io/gate-result/v1. Fields: gate_id, result (PASS/FAIL/ADVISORY/NOT_APPLICABLE), policy_hash, input_hash, timestamp (ISO 8601 UTC), runner, commit_sha, optional metadata. Validates synthetic test rows. Draft 2020-12.
close_reason: JSON Schema gate-result.schema.json authored. Draft 2020-12. Validates required fields (gate_id with regex, result enum, policy_hash + input_hash with sha256 prefix pattern, timestamp date-time, runner with semver pattern, commit_sha 7-40 hex). Optional metadata, failure_mode, advisory_severity. Conditional: ADVISORY result requires advisory_severity.

description:
Implement Rollout Gate GHA. Lives at intent-eval-lab/gha/rollout-gate/action.yml. Reads Evidence Bundle, evaluates OPA policy, emits ship/no-ship status to $GITHUB_OUTPUT.

v0.1.0 scope: minimal (read bundle, load policy, evaluate, emit decision). Out of scope v0.1.0: Argo Rollouts, canary analysis, progressive delivery.

Master plan: ~/.claude/plans/please-take-your-time-glimmering-stardust.md
Planning ID: IEL-9
Parent umbrella: IEL-CONV-6 (Rollout Gate GHA)
Phase: C
Depends on: IEL-7 (CLI verifies bundles)
notes:
GitHub: jeremylongshore/intent-eval-lab#20 — https://github.com/jeremylongshore/intent-eval-lab/issues/20. MIRROR RULE: bd-sync handles fan-out — `bd-sync note iel-z3q` and `bd-sync close iel-z3q` mirror to GH and Plane automatically.

Plane: LAB-30 — https://projects.intentsolutions.io/internal/projects/?peekIssue=LAB-30

title: Verify placeholder module READMEs are vendor-neutral (no scrubbing needed)
description:
Verify three placeholder modules (validator-contract-reliability, forecasting-drift-detection, decentralized-crypto-evaluation) describe failure-mode classes generically without naming any specific commercial entity.

Verification result (2026-05-10): Already vendor-neutral. All three placeholder READMEs describe domain shapes generically with no named-partner references.

Adjacent scrub: while verifying placeholders, an audit of mcp-plugin-observability/ surfaced vendor-named references in the in-progress first case study. Per partner-consent discipline (a partner is not named publicly until written consent is on file), those references were scrubbed 2026-05-10. The case study now lives only in the engagement's private docs until consent is given.
